page_summary: A page from an occult or grimoire-style text featuring a large triangular magic diagram of numbered interconnected circles, surrounded by paragraphs of text written in Theban (Witch's/Honorian) script.

decorations.description: A large triangular magic-square diagram composed of numbered circles (numbers roughly 121–214) connected by straight black lines, forming a nested series of concentric triangles and a grid-like interior. The apex is at the top with circle 131, and the diagram widens downward to a base containing circles such as 188, 193, 142, 187, 144, 156, 164, 121, 211, 170.
decorations.position: center of the page, between upper and lower text blocks
components: lines connecting each circle to multiple neighbors forming a triangular lattice
bottom base of circles forming the wide base of the triangle

uncertain_or_unreadable: The script in the text blocks is Theban (Honorian/Witch's alphabet) and cannot be reliably transcribed into Latin letters from the image alone; the character transcriptions above are best-effort approximations of the glyph shapes only.
The exact connection pattern of the lower circles in the triangular diagram is partially obscured and hard to trace.
